title: The Hardest Way to Make a GIF
excerpt: Everyone loves a good GIF, and my favourite (of all time) is this one (circa 2015):
content_html:
Everyone loves a good GIF, and my favourite (of all time) is this one (circa 2015):

Let’s make a GIF in the hardest way possible.

Why? It’s pointless, but some pointless things are just fun to do.

Step 1: Acquire photons

For this step I’ll be using the Lomo ActionSampler, a plastic camera made in China by a series of anonymous unknown factories. It’s been branded as many different things over the years, including as the Revolver Selby in 1991, so it’s a design that’s at least 35 years old.

The camera uses a unique clockwork spring-powered mechanism to rotate a circular shutter over 4 different lenses, at a speed of around 1/250s per frame. This means, if you’re lucky, you can capture 1 second of action at a glorious 4 frames per second.

Because you never really know how the photos will turn out, I’ll shoot a 36 exposure roll of Lucky SHD 400 film, and we’ll see what we get. Now that our photos are captured on China’s finest cheapest black and white film, we need to make them visible.

Step 2: Develop film

I could send the film off to a lab for developing and scanning, but this is the hardest way to make a GIF. I develop film at home so luckily I have all the requisite parts:

  • Developer tank and reel
  • Ilford Ilfosol 3 developer
  • Ilford Rapidfix fixer
  • A dark bag
  • Jugs, thermometer and other assorted accessories

By a combination of luck and limited skill, it worked! We have developed film, each frame containing 4 images captured at 4 frames per second.

Step 3: Scan images

I don’t have a fancy film scanner, so for this I’ll be mounting each frame on an LED light panel then taking a photo with a digital camera on a tripod, with lots of light and a small aperture to try to get the best detail possible. I’ll shoot the frames in RAW which will give me some flexibility in the software later to expose them more correctly. This is similar to the digital intermediary phase in “real” filmmaking.

I loaded the RAW files into Affinity and eyeballed the exposure to hopefully get useful images back. They’re inverted of course, so a quick cmd+i inverts them the right way round.

Step 4: Crop frames

The alignment of each 4 frames within the 35mm frame is not exact (this is a plastic camera from the 1990s after all). In fact frames 1 and 2 are a different width to frames 3 and 4, so I’ll need to crop each frame, then align them as layers manually in Affinity. Making the frames 50% transparency helps as a rudimentary form of onion skinning.

Now we export the frames as PNGs, and number them by frame order.

Step 5: Align frames and Gif-ify

Using imagemagick on the command line I was able to combine the images into a GIF. I went for a “ping pong” animation style, just to make the GIF last a little longer and give a better sense of the motion.

magick -delay 25 -loop 0 1.png 2.png 3.png 4.png 3.png 2.png animated.gif

Delay 25 means 25/100ths of a second, or 4fps - making it more lifelike to the real world it was captured from.

Naturally with 4fps this isn’t exactly like watching a Pixar movie, so I created some tweens by blending frames at 50% opacity each using:

magick 1.png 2.png -define compose:args=50,50 -compose blend -composite 1-2-tween.png

With these tween frames as part of the animation it appears much smoother.

And there you have it! A looping animated GIF, acquired from the real world on 35mm film. Next project: do it with IMAX. ;)

Here are some of my favourites:

content_html_zh:
每个人都喜欢精美的 GIF，而我（一直以来）最喜欢的是这个（大约 2015 年）：

让我们用最难的方式制作一个 GIF。

为什么？ 虽然没什么意义，但有些无意义的事情却很有趣。

第 1 步：获取光子

在这一步中，我将使用 Lomo ActionSampler，这是一款由一系列不知名工厂在中国制造的塑料相机。 多年来，它被赋予了许多不同的品牌，包括 1991 年的 Revolver Selby，所以它的设计至少有 35 年的历史。

该相机采用独特的发条弹簧驱动机构，以每帧约 1/250 秒的速度在 4 个不同的镜头上旋转圆形快门。 这意味着，如果幸运的话，您可以以每秒 4 帧的速度捕捉 1 秒的动作。

因为你永远不知道照片会变成什么样子，所以我会用 Lucky SHD 400 胶片拍摄 36 曝光卷，然后看看会得到什么。 既然我们的照片是用中国最好、最便宜的黑白胶片拍摄的，我们需要让它们可见。

第 2 步：冲洗胶片

我可以将胶片送到实验室进行冲洗和扫描，但这是制作 GIF 最难的方法。 我在家冲洗胶片，幸运的是我拥有所有必需的部件：

  • 显影槽和卷轴
  • 伊尔福德 Ilfosol 3 开发商
  • 伊尔福德 Rapidfix 固定器
  • 一个深色的袋子
  • 水壶、温度计和其他各种配件

运气和有限的技术相结合，它成功了！ 我们开发了胶片，每帧包含 4 个以每秒 4 帧的速度拍摄的图像。

第 3 步：扫描图像

我没有精美的胶片扫描仪，因此为此我将把每个画面安装在 LED 灯板上，然后用三脚架上的数码相机拍照，在充足的光线和小光圈的情况下尝试获得最佳细节。 我将以 RAW 格式拍摄帧，这将给我稍后使用软件提供一些灵活性，以便更正确地曝光它们。 这类似于“真实”电影制作中的数字中介阶段。

我将 RAW 文件加载到 Affinity 中并观察曝光情况，希望能得到有用的图像。 当然，它们是颠倒的，因此快速 cmd+i 可以将它们颠倒过来。

第 4 步：裁剪框架

35mm 画幅内每 4 个画框的对齐并不精确（毕竟这是 20 世纪 90 年代的塑料相机）。 事实上，第 1 帧和第 2 帧与第 3 帧和第 4 帧的宽度不同，因此我需要裁剪每个帧，然后在 Affinity 中手动将它们作为图层对齐。 使框架具有 50% 的透明度有助于作为洋葱皮的基本形式。

现在我们将帧导出为 PNG，并按帧顺序对它们进行编号。

第 5 步：对齐框架并 Gif-ify

在命令行上使用 imagemagick 我能够将图像组合成 GIF。 我选择了“乒乓”动画风格，只是为了让 GIF 持续时间更长一些，并提供更好的运动感。

magick -delay 25 -loop 0 1.png 2.png 3.png 4.png 3.png 2.png animated.gif

延迟 25 意味着 25/100 秒，或 4 fps - 使其更接近所捕获的现实世界。

当然，对于 4fps，这并不完全像观看皮克斯电影，因此我通过以 50% 不透明度混合帧来创建一些补间，每个帧使用：

magick 1.png 2.png -define compose:args=50,50 -compose blend -composite 1-2-tween.png

使用这些补间帧作为动画的一部分，它看起来更加平滑。

现在你就拥有了！ 循环动画 GIF，从现实世界获取 35 毫米胶片。 下一个项目：用 IMAX 来做。 ;)

以下是我最喜欢的一些：
